title: [InstCombine] Fold `ucmp/scmp(x, y) >> N` to `zext/sext(x < y)` when N is one less than the width of the result of `ucmp/scmp`

tests.outside_scope_reason: Existing tests only cover i8 cmp result type; this uses i16 result type with shift by 15 (bitwidth-1 for i16)

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i16 @scmp_i16_result_ashr(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = scmp i16 i32 %x, %y
  %#range_0_%#1 = !range i16 %#1, i16 65535, i16 2
  %#2 = ashr i16 %#range_0_%#1, 15
  ret i16 %#2
}
=>
define i16 @scmp_i16_result_ashr(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = icmp slt i32 %x, %y
  %#2 = sext i1 %#1 to i16
  ret i16 %#2
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Existing tests only cover i8 cmp result type; this uses i32 result type with shift by 31 (bitwidth-1 for i32)

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @ucmp_i32_result_lshr(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = ucmp i32 i32 %x, %y
  %#range_0_%#1 = !range i32 %#1, i32 4294967295, i32 2
  %#2 = lshr i32 %#range_0_%#1, 31
  ret i32 %#2
}
=>
define i32 @ucmp_i32_result_lshr(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = icmp ult i32 %x, %y
  %#2 = zext i1 %#1 to i32
  ret i32 %#2
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Existing tests only use i32 comparison operands; this uses i64 operands while maintaining i8 result type

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i8 @scmp_i64_operands_ashr(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = scmp i8 i64 %x, %y
  %#range_0_%#1 = !range i8 %#1, i8 255, i8 2
  %#2 = ashr i8 %#range_0_%#1, 7
  ret i8 %#2
}
=>
define i8 @scmp_i64_operands_ashr(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = icmp slt i64 %x, %y
  %#2 = sext i1 %#1 to i8
  ret i8 %#2
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
